compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — criterion T1 (prior-art / novel-architecture justification) [overlay]: PASS, no findings.

Although this plan crosses a T1 bright-line (a novel-sounding "observability substrate" / reducer-ignored sidecar), it does NOT reinvent or repackage — it explicitly grounds every architectural choice in established in-repo prior art, and that prior art checks out against the code:

- Reducer-ignored / preserved-and-ignored event ← the existing forward-compat machinery in `src/rebar/reducer/_version.py` (`KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES`, `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES`, `is_unknown_newer_type`). `REVIEW_RESULT` is already in `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES`.
- The plan names the SYNC / PRECONDITIONS precedent as the pattern it mirrors — confirmed in `_version.py:42-44` and `sidecar.py:13`.
- fsck WARN on event types newer than the binary ← existing `is_unknown_newer_type` used by `fsck`/`bridge_fsck` (`src/rebar/_engine_support/bridge_fsck.py:77-86`).
- Retention/cleanup "reuse rebar's existing event compaction" — an explicit decision to reuse, not build new (compaction preserves forward-compat payload per `_version.py:14-16`).
- Single-owner caller-visible finding id ← `orchestrator.mint_finding_id` is already the SOLE owner (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py:152-158`); the plan has the sidecar RECORD and the coach REFERENCE it (reference-not-restate), avoiding circular alignment.
- readers-before-writers rollout ← the established preserved-and-ignored-by-older-clones pattern.

Novelty (append-only sidecar per run vs idempotent head-state attestation) is justified explicitly in the Scope section. Capability assertions are resolved against real code rather than left as unverified claims. ANTI-FP applies — no finding warranted.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 review — rubric T11 (Data-migration / backfill safety, overlay):

NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS — no findings.

T11 is an overlay that fires only when a plan changes a schema/persisted format or backfills data; its binary checks are migration-EXECUTION concerns (online/expand-contract DDL vs. a blocking lock on a large table; batched/throttled backfills; resumability/idempotency of a partial migration; dual-write window; rollback/no-data-loss on partial failure). The overlay's ANTI-FP clause makes it not-applicable for non-persisted/in-memory or non-backfill changes.

Why it does not fire here (grounded in the plan's own text + the established rebar event model):

(a) NO BACKFILL / NO DATA MIGRATION. The plan is explicitly "Capture only" (Scope) and "intentionally APPEND-ONLY per review run". It adds a NEW reducer-ignored event type (REVIEW_RESULT) to rebar's append-only, file-per-event git-backed log — there are no pre-existing rows/events to rewrite, transform, or backfill. Each review simply appends one new event going forward. There is no expand-contract sequence, no add-nullable→backfill→enforce, because there is no existing persisted record to migrate.

(b) NO BLOCKING DDL ON A LARGE TABLE. rebar's store is an append-only event log, not a relational table — no DDL, no table lock, no single-shot blocking migration. The T11 MAJOR trigger (irreversible single-shot migration / long blocking lock on a large table) has no surface here.

(c) The schema-evolution concern that DOES exist — an older clone meeting the new event type — is handled by rebar's existing PRESERVED-AND-IGNORED forward-compat mechanism, which the plan explicitly reuses ("an older clone … PRESERVES-and-IGNORES the event"; "reuse rebar's existing event compaction"). That maps to rollout/breakage-acknowledgement (T4 family), not to T11's data-migration-execution checks. Confirmed in the codebase: REVIEW_RESULT is registered in `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES` and the write allow-list with no transformation of prior data (src/rebar/reducer/_version.py:44; src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py:1–14), and compaction preserves unknown/forward-compat event files rather than rewriting them (forward-compat contract tested in tests/interfaces/contracts/test_event_schema_forward_compat.py).

(d) The RETENTION/CLEANUP path (Scope; AC#4) bounds growth by reusing existing age/size compaction/archival — bounded growth of an append-only log, not a backfill of existing data; T11's batching/resumability/dual-write checks address migrating existing rows and do not apply.

Affirmations (T11): The plan's own text affirmatively establishes a purely additive, append-only, capture-only feature with no persisted-format rewrite and no backfill, and explicitly defers schema-evolution safety to the established preserved-and-ignored pattern + existing compaction. Under the overlay gate and ANTI-FP clause, T11 is not-applicable — clean, no findings.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 review — T3 (Integration feasibility) [overlay]: NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS.

The overlay applies only when a plan integrates an external API/CLI/service/library or asserts a never-before-used capability. This plan integrates only internal, already-used rebar mechanisms — the reducer skip-set, compaction's preserve-filter, the write-path allow-list, fsck's newer-type WARN, and the preserve-and-ignored-by-older-clones rollout pattern — so per the overlay's gating clause and ANTI-FP rule ("an internal, already-used integration is not-applicable") it does not engage.

Affirmations (criteria satisfied, recorded as positive feedback, not findings):
- The integration is achievable as described — every named capability is present in-tree: REVIEW_RESULT is registered in _NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES (src/rebar/reducer/_version.py:44) and the write allow-list (src/rebar/_store/event_append.py:63); the reducer-skip relies on REVIEW_RESULT being absent from KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES (_version.py:65); the fsck WARN path is real (src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py:170 and src/rebar/_engine_support/bridge_fsck.py:120 both call is_unknown_newer_type). No capability gap.
- Named subcommands/endpoints exist — MATCH (internal): the SYNC/PRECONDITIONS/TAG_DELTA preserve-and-ignore precedent the plan cites is implemented; sidecar emit + payload assembly already exist (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py).
- Auth/HTTPS preconditions — not-applicable: no external/network surface is introduced.
- SPIKE-on-critical-gap — not-applicable: no critical capability gap exists, so no SPIKE is warranted.

No T3 findings.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — G6 (approach soundness / anti-patterns / alternative-selection): CLEAN, no findings.

Affirmations (criteria passed, grounded in code):
- MECHANISM CORRECTNESS: the reducer-ignored sidecar mechanism is logic/data-flow complete and verified end-to-end against the codebase — REVIEW_RESULT is absent from KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES (reducer/_version.py) so the reducer skips it; present in the write allow-list (_store/event_append.py:63) so it can be emitted; compaction preserve-filter (_commands/compact.py:74-85) skips unknown-type events untouched (never snapshotted/deleted); listed in _NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES (_version.py:44) so fsck does not false-warn. The "PRESERVES enough recent history" + "reuse existing event compaction" claims hold. Failure case handled: emit() is best-effort and never fails the review.
- No TOCTOU / hidden-atomicity hazard: the append-only sidecar is intentionally non-idempotent (every run recorded); idempotency lives on the SIGNATURE attestation whose manifest is timestamp-free/deterministic (attest.py:27-31), so re-signing the same verified head-state is reproducible — one current valid signature, not duplicates. The split is sound.
- No circular id alignment: the content-fingerprint finding `id` has a single owner — the orchestrator (ca03) at result assembly (orchestrator.py:18,152-154); the sidecar RECORDS that id and the Pass-4 coach references it (reference-not-restate). One owner, no restatement loop.
- FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE: solves the named problem (offline per-criterion FP/FN reconstruction) directly, not a proxy — passive capture off the hot paths.
- APPROACH SELECTION: considered two structurally-different alternatives — (a) a reduced compiled-state field, (b) an external analytics store. The chosen reducer-ignored event is defensibly at-least-as-good on codebase-alignment (reuses the existing forward-compat + compaction substrate, mirrors the SYNC/PRECONDITIONS precedent), blast-radius (hot paths untouched), and simplicity. No clearly-superior alternative missed.
- POSITIVE RATIONALE present: "without taxing rebar's hot paths" and "reuse rebar's existing event compaction" / preserved-and-ignored-by-older-clones rollout.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: PASS 1 — T1 (Prior-art / novel-architecture justification): PASS. The plan explicitly reuses established in-codebase patterns rather than inventing new architecture: the reducer-ignored sidecar mirrors the SYNC/PRECONDITIONS precedent (_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES in reducer/_version.py:44), retention reuses "rebar's existing event compaction", and rollout follows the documented "preserved-and-ignored-by-older-clones" forward-compat pattern (reducer/_version.py:8-19, CLAUDE.md:301). No bright-line novel pattern fires; no unverified capability assertions.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 review — rubric T11 (Data-migration / backfill safety, overlay):

NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS — no findings. T11 is an overlay that fires only when the plan changes a persisted schema/format in-place or backfills existing data; it is migration-EXECUTION safety. This plan does neither.

Grounded reasoning:
- ADD-ONLY, NO IN-PLACE SCHEMA CHANGE: the deliverable is a NEW reducer-IGNORED REVIEW_RESULT event type ("the reducer skips the type") plus an attestation head-state event. It is "intentionally APPEND-ONLY per review run." No existing event format is altered in place and no large persisted table is re-shaped, so there is no blocking DDL / expand-contract concern to satisfy.
- NO BACKFILL: the plan captures data going FORWARD only ("Capture only; analysis is offline"). There is no migration that reads historical rows and rewrites/populates them, so batching/throttling, resumability/idempotent re-run, dual-write window, and partial-failure rollback (T11 a–e) have no surface to apply to. The append-only, non-idempotent-by-design capture is a deliberate observability choice, not a backfill that risks double-applying.
- FORWARD-COMPAT IS HANDLED ELSEWHERE IN THE RUBRIC: the rollout AC ("preserved-and-ignored-by-older-clones," fsck WARNs on newer event types, readers-before-writers) is event-version coexistence, distinct from data-migration execution; it does not pull T11 into scope.
- RETENTION/CLEANUP is compaction/archival of the new sidecar ("reuse rebar's existing event compaction"), not a migration of existing persisted data.

ANTI-FP: T11 is explicitly not-applicable for additive/non-rewriting persistence changes. This is an add-a-new-event-type-going-forward change with no historical data rewrite, so the overlay does not apply.

Affirmations (T11): not-applicable for this additive, append-only, forward-capture-only sidecar — no migration/backfill of existing persisted data is introduced; no findings.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — rubric T10 (Infrastructure / IaC, overlay):

NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS — no findings. T10 is an overlay that applies only when a plan provisions or configures infrastructure (cloud resources; IaC via Terraform/CloudFormation/CDK/Pulumi/Ansible; Kubernetes/Helm). This ticket is a reducer-IGNORED REVIEW_RESULT event recorded into rebar's EXISTING event log — a software/data-layer observability substrate. No infra is provisioned or configured:

- STATE/PLAN-APPLY, IAM, SECRETS, ENV-ISOLATION, COST/SIZING, OBSERVABILITY-AS-INFRA: none apply — there is no remote state, no cloud roles/policies, no provisioned resources, no secrets-in-IaC surface.
- RETENTION/CLEANUP (Scope, T9/T5b) is implemented by REUSING "rebar's existing event compaction" — in-repo software behavior over the append-only event log, not infrastructure provisioning. (The COST captured in the new latency/cost AC is per-pass review cost recorded as sidecar metadata, not cloud-resource cost/sizing.)
- ROLLOUT / cross-clone coexistence AC concerns event-format/binary forward-compat (preserve-and-ignore by older clones, fsck WARN on newer event types, readers-before-writers) — not Helm/IaC env-isolation or blast-radius.

Per the overlay's ANTI-FP clause (not-applicable for non-infra tickets), T10 does not fire.

Affirmations (T10): not-applicable for this non-infra, event-log software ticket — no findings.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 review — rubric T11 (Data-migration / backfill safety, overlay): NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS — no findings.

T11 is an overlay (migration-EXECUTION safety) that fires only when a plan changes a persisted schema/format in-place or backfills existing data. This plan does neither, grounded in its own text + the established rebar event model:

- ADDITIVE, NO IN-PLACE SCHEMA REWRITE: the deliverable is a NEW reducer-IGNORED `REVIEW_RESULT` event type ("the reducer skips the type") + an attestation head-state event, "intentionally APPEND-ONLY per review run." No existing event/record format is rewritten. T11(a) expand-contract / add-nullable→backfill→enforce / blocking-DDL has no surface.

- NO BACKFILL: Scope = "Capture only; analysis is offline." Data is captured FORWARD only; no historical events are read-and-repopulated. So T11(b) batching/throttling, (c) resumability/idempotent re-run, (d) dual-write window, (e) partial-failure rollback have nothing to apply to. (The deliberate append-only/non-idempotent capture is an observability design choice — not a backfill at risk of double-apply; idempotency correctly lives on the attestation head-state.)

- NO BLOCKING DDL ON A LARGE TABLE: rebar's store is an append-only, file-per-event git log, not a relational table — no DDL, no table lock, no single-shot blocking migration. The T11 MAJOR trigger (irreversible single-shot migration / long blocking lock) has no surface.

- SCHEMA-EVOLUTION ≠ DATA-MIGRATION: the older-clone-meets-new-type concern (rollout AC: preserved-and-ignored, fsck WARN on newer types, readers-before-writers) is event-version coexistence (T4 family), not T11 migration-execution. Confirmed in code: `REVIEW_RESULT` ∈ `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES` (src/rebar/reducer/_version.py:44) and compaction preserves unknown/forward-compat event files byte-equivalently without rewriting prior data (tests/interfaces/contracts/test_event_schema_forward_compat.py:110-148).

- RETENTION/CLEANUP (Scope; AC#4) bounds growth via existing age/size compaction/archival of the NEW sidecar — bounded growth of an append-only log, not a backfill of existing rows.

ANTI-FP: T11 is explicitly not-applicable for additive/non-rewriting persistence changes with no backfill.

Affirmations (T11): The plan's own text affirmatively establishes a purely additive, append-only, forward-capture-only feature with no persisted-format rewrite and no backfill of existing data; schema-evolution safety is correctly deferred to the established preserved-and-ignored pattern + existing compaction. Under the overlay gate + ANTI-FP clause, T11 is not-applicable — clean, no findings.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — T1 (Prior-art / novel-architecture justification) [overlay]: PASS, no findings.

This plan does cross a T1 bright-line (it reads as a novel "observability substrate" / reducer-ignored event pattern), so the overlay engages. But it does NOT reinvent or repackage — it explicitly grounds every architectural choice in established in-repo prior art, and that prior art checks out against the code:

(a) Relevant prior art considered (not reinventing):
- Reducer-ignored / preserved-and-ignored event ← existing forward-compat machinery in src/rebar/reducer/_version.py (KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES, _NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES, is_unknown_newer_type). REVIEW_RESULT is already registered in _NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES (_version.py:44).
- The plan names the SYNC / PRECONDITIONS precedent as the pattern it mirrors — confirmed in _version.py:42-44 and sidecar.py:13.
- "reuse rebar's existing event compaction" — an explicit decision to reuse, not build new (compaction preserves forward-compat payload per _version.py:14-16).
- fsck WARN on event types newer than the binary ← existing is_unknown_newer_type used by fsck/bridge_fsck.
- readers-before-writers rollout ← the documented preserved-and-ignored-by-older-clones forward-compat pattern.

(b) Novelty justified vs an established approach (anti-repackaging / Rule-of-Three): the only genuinely new decision — append-only sidecar PER RUN (non-idempotent by design) vs idempotency living on the head-state attestation — is justified explicitly in Scope ("every run recorded for analysis"; idempotency on the SIGNATURE attestation whose manifest is deterministic, attest.py:27-31). The single-owner finding id (orchestrator.mint_finding_id, the sole owner per orchestrator.py:152-158; sidecar RECORDS, coach REFERENCES — reference-not-restate) avoids a circular-alignment anti-pattern. No new architecture is invented where an existing rebar mechanism suffices.

(c) Unverified capability assertions resolved: every "rebar supports X" claim is verifiable in-tree rather than asserted (reducer-skip, write allow-list, compaction preserve-filter, fsck newer-type WARN all exist). No unresolved capability assertion.

ANTI-FP applies for the reuse cases — they are well-trodden in-repo patterns needing no external prior-art search. No T1 finding warranted.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 review — T3 (Integration feasibility) [overlay]: NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS.

T3 applies only when a plan integrates an external API/CLI/service/library or asserts a never-before-used capability. This plan integrates exclusively with rebar's internal, already-used event-store machinery — no external surface is introduced. Per T3's ANTI-FP clause ("an internal, already-used integration is not-applicable"), this is a clean PASS.

Every capability the plan asserts was verified present and exercised in-tree, so none is "asserted-but-unverified":
- Reducer-skip / forward-compat: `KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES` + `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES` already lists `REVIEW_RESULT` (src/rebar/reducer/_version.py:44).
- Compaction PRESERVES the event: documented (src/rebar/reducer/_version.py:14-16) and pinned by test (tests/interfaces/contracts/test_event_schema_forward_compat.py:110 `test_unknown_event_file_survives_compaction`).
- `fsck` WARN on event types newer than the running binary: `is_unknown_newer_type` (src/rebar/reducer/_version.py:47) + Check 5 (src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py:152-175).
- Preserved-and-ignored-by-older-clones rollout, readers-before-writers: existing rebar pattern (CLAUDE.md:301; docs/event-schema.md:77).

No capability gap; no SPIKE warranted on T3 grounds. Affirmations: technical_feasibility (achievable as described, on existing primitives); named mechanisms MATCH the codebase; auth/HTTPS preconditions n/a (no external endpoint). No T3 findings.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 REVIEW (G6 — approach soundness). One grounded finding + affirmations below.

FINDING [G6 — mechanism correctness, retention/cleanup edge case]
- location: Scope → "RETENTION/CLEANUP (T9/T5b)" + AC-4 ("A retention/cleanup path bounds sidecar growth … reuse rebar's existing event compaction")
- criteria: [G6]
- evidence: The plan's retention mechanism says to "reuse rebar's existing event compaction" to bound sidecar growth. But rebar's existing compaction does the OPPOSITE for this sidecar: `src/rebar/_commands/compact.py:74-85` explicitly *skips* (preserves untouched, never snapshots or deletes) any event whose type is not in `KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES`, and `REVIEW_RESULT` is intentionally NOT in `KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES` (`src/rebar/reducer/_version.py:65-94`; it lives only in `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES` + the write allow-list). So the named "reuse existing compaction" mechanism cannot bound `REVIEW_RESULT` growth — by design it preserves these events forever (the forward-compat preserve rule documented at `_version.py:9-17`). AC-4 therefore names a mechanism that, as it exists in the code, does not achieve AC-4's own goal.
- checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile AC-4's retention mechanism with the live preserve-filter: rebar's existing compaction PRESERVES (never deletes/snapshots) reducer-ignored types like `REVIEW_RESULT` (`compact.py:74-85`, `_version.py:9-17`), so it cannot bound sidecar growth as written. Specify the ACTUAL bounding path — e.g. an age/size-based archival/prune step distinct from compaction (which deliberately retains forward-compat payload), and state how it preserves recent history for offline FP/FN analysis.
- suggested_fix: Replace "reuse rebar's existing event compaction" with an explicit, separate retention path (age/size-windowed archival or prune of old `REVIEW_RESULT` files), and call out that this is intentionally NOT the compaction-into-SNAPSHOT path (which must keep preserving the type for forward-compat). If the intent is genuinely "archive-to-analytics, never delete in place," say so and drop the word "compaction."

AFFIRMATIONS (G6 PASS on the remaining checks):
- Mechanism (core): the reducer-IGNORED sidecar — out of compiled state / deps / validate / hot paths, recognized by fsck (no "newer-than-me" warn), preserved by compaction — is logic-complete and matches the live code (`_version.py:44-54`, `event_append.py:60-64`, `sidecar.py:1-15`). Append-only-by-design with idempotency on the attestation head-state (one current valid signature, not duplicates) is internally consistent and avoids a check-then-act race on the sidecar.
- Single-owner id flow: orchestrator `ca03` is the SOLE minter via content fingerprint (`orchestrator.py:152-158 mint_finding_id`), sidecar RECORDS it, coach REFERENCES it — non-circular, no alignment race. Sound.
- Fitness-for-purpose: capture-only sidecar + offline replay/join solves the named problem (offline FP/remediation without taxing hot paths) directly, not a proxy.
- Approach selection: reviewer-generated alternatives (fold verdicts into compiled state via COMMENT/EDIT; or a separate out-of-band analytics store) are both worse on hot-path blast-radius and codebase-alignment than the reducer-ignored sidecar, which reuses the established SYNC/PRECONDITIONS forward-compat precedent. Chosen approach is defensibly at-least-as-good — PASS.
- Positive rationale: present ("without taxing rebar's hot paths"; "preserved-and-ignored-by-older-clones pattern"; readers-before-writers rollout). No absence finding.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: PASS 1 review — criterion T1 (Prior-art / novel-architecture justification): PASS, no findings. The T1 bright-line fires (new REVIEW_RESULT event type as an observability substrate), but the plan explicitly grounds itself in established in-repo prior art rather than inventing a novel architecture: it reuses the existing reducer-ignored sidecar pattern (REVIEW_RESULT is already in docs/reuse-surface.md and _NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES / docs/event-schema.md), "reuse[s] rebar's existing event compaction" for retention, and the rollout AC follows "rebar's preserved-and-ignored-by-older-clones pattern" (the documented forward-compat mechanism in docs/event-schema.md and tests/interfaces/contracts/test_event_schema_forward_compat.py). No unverified external/library capability assertions. Anti-repackaging and prior-art checks satisfied.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: PASS-1 review — T3 (Integration feasibility) [overlay]: NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS. The plan integrates only rebar's own pre-existing internal event-log substrate (reducer-skip of unknown types, compaction preserve-filter, fsck recognition of non-replay known types, write-path allow-list, attestation head-state). These are already-used internal mechanisms confirmed in the codebase (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py, reducer/_version.py, _store/event_append.py, _commands/compact.py, and the forward-compat tests). No external API/CLI/service/library and no asserted-but-unused capability — the overlay does not trigger. No findings.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 review — rubric T8 (LLM/prompt structural-completeness probe, overlay)

This overlay applies: the plan defines part of an LLM/agent review system (a REVIEW_RESULT output schema, a per-criterion verdict vocabulary, finding fingerprints, and references to two sibling agents — the orchestrator `ca03` and the Pass-4 coach `75a9`). I tool-grounded the referenced agents/enums/schema and found ONE proven structural gap (the latency/cost metadata field) plus several criteria that affirmatively PASS.

FINDINGS

- [ ] Define the per-pass LATENCY + COST metadata shape the new AC requires — schema location, value vocabulary, units, and per-pass keying (T8: schema/enum field referenced but its value vocabulary never defined).
  - criteria: T8
  - location: Plan §"Acceptance Criteria (added — close G3 coverage)", AC "Capture per-pass LATENCY + COST … in the sidecar metadata"
  - evidence: The AC names a metadata field ("per-pass LATENCY + COST", distinguishing "the fast claim-path check" vs "the out-of-band review") but never defines its shape. The implemented sidecar payload (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py:68-82`) and the verdict schema (`src/rebar/schemas/plan_review_verdict.schema.json:46-50`) carry only a free-form `coverage` object (`additionalProperties: true`) describing "what ran" — no latency or cost field. `docs/plan-review-gate.md:183-184` enumerates the captured fields as "per-finding fingerprints + decisions + verification attributes + coverage" — again no latency/cost. An executor cannot tell WHERE these live (a new top-level field? a `coverage.latency`/`coverage.cost` sub-key?), the UNITS (wall-clock ms? tokens? USD?), or how the two passes are KEYED so offline join can separate the fast-path check from the out-of-band review. This is the T8 pattern: a metadata vocabulary referenced by an AC but never specified, so the "refined PASSIVELY from dogfood data" promise has no defined substrate. (criteria_routing.json:336/348 define `latency`/`cost` only as T5a/cost-sizing rubric trigger keys — unrelated to a sidecar metadata schema.)
  - suggested_fix: Add a `metrics` (or `coverage.metrics`) object to the sidecar payload schema with explicit per-pass keys and units, e.g. `{"pass": "det-floor"|"pass1-find"|"pass2-verify"|"pass3-decide"|"pass4-coach"|"claim-path-check", "wall_ms": int, "tokens": {"in": int, "out": int}, "usd": float|null}`, and state that the fast claim-path check and the out-of-band review are recorded as distinct keyed entries so offline analysis can separate them.

AFFIRMATIONS (criteria this chunk passes under T8)

- No undefined-but-referenced enum: the per-criterion VERDICT vocabulary the sidecar records is fully defined — `verdict` is PASS|BLOCK|INDETERMINATE (`plan_review_verdict.schema.json:10-13`), and the per-finding fields the sidecar slims (`id`, `criteria`, `tier`, `decision`, `severity`, `validity`, `impact`, `priority`, `verification`) are enumerated in `sidecar.py:47-59`. "finding fingerprints" resolves to the content-fingerprint `id` minted by `orchestrator.mint_finding_id` (`orchestrator.py:152-154`).

- Referenced agents exist and are fully specified (tool-verified): the orchestrator `ca03` (dry-frost-zenith, CLOSED) is the SOLE owner that MINTS the finding `id`, and the Pass-4 coach `75a9` (crusty-herb-stand) CONSUMES it via `finding_refs`. The id-ownership protocol is unambiguous and non-circular — `ca03`'s What and `75a9`'s "Caller-visible finding id contract" both state mint-once/consume, matching `orchestrator.py:18` ("SOLE owner") and `sidecar.py` (records the same id). No pink-elephant / instruction-locality antipattern: the sidecar correctly RECORDS the id and does not re-mint it.

- No counter/state increment with ambiguous placement: idempotency placement is explicitly disambiguated — the sidecar is APPEND-ONLY per run (NOT idempotent, by design), while idempotency lives on the ATTESTATION head-state (one current valid signature). This is the kind of state-placement spec T8 looks for, and it is present.

- A fallback for a failed sub-step is specified: the sidecar emit is best-effort and "a failed emit must NEVER fail the review itself" (`sidecar.py:24-37` returns False on any exception) — the observability substrate degrades without taxing the hot path, consistent with the plan's "without taxing rebar's hot paths" intent.

- The rollout/coexistence protocol referenced by the added AC is real and co-located with the schema: `is_unknown_newer_type("REVIEW_RESULT") is False` and `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES = {"SYNC","PRECONDITIONS","REVIEW_RESULT"}` (`reducer/_version.py:44-53`); fsck WARNs on truly-newer types (`_commands/fsck.py:161-170`); the preserve-and-ignore + readers-before-writers rule is documented at `sidecar.py:7-14`. No structural gap here.

Net: one MAJOR-candidate structural gap (undefined latency/cost metadata vocabulary on a referenced schema field); the rest of the T8 surface is clean.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 review (G6 — Approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection): PASS, no findings.

Mechanism verified against live code:
- Reducer-ignored: `REVIEW_RESULT` is correctly absent from `KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES` (src/rebar/reducer/_version.py:65-94) so the reducer skips it and it never enters compiled state / deps / validate / hot paths — and the SAME single-source-of-truth set drives the compaction preserve-filter, so the file is preserved-and-ignored, never folded into a SNAPSHOT (documented at _version.py:8-19). This is the documented forward-compat substrate, not a hidden assumption.
- fsck does NOT mis-warn "newer than me": `REVIEW_RESULT` is in `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES` (_version.py:44) which `is_unknown_newer_type` consults (_version.py:47-54), satisfying the rollout AC.
- Emittable but harmless: it is in the write-path allow-list (src/rebar/_store/event_append.py:60-63) yet outside the replay dispatch — the exact SYNC/PRECONDITIONS precedent the plan cites.
- Best-effort / append-only by design: emit() swallows all exceptions and returns False so a sidecar failure never fails the review (sidecar.py:24-37); idempotency correctly lives on the ATTESTATION head-state (attest.py: manifest has no timestamps, "re-signing the same verified state is reproducible", attest.py:27-41) — so the plan's "append-only sidecar + idempotent attestation" split is internally consistent, no TOCTOU.
- Single id owner: `mint_finding_id` is the sole content-fingerprint owner in the orchestrator (orchestrator.py:152-158); the sidecar RECORDS the same id (sidecar.py:_slim → f.get("id")). No circular alignment — the plan's "one owner, reference-not-restate" claim holds.

Approach selection: I weighed two structurally-different alternatives and discarded both as inferior — (a) a KNOWN reducer-folded event into compiled_state taxes the hot paths the plan explicitly protects and enlarges blast-radius; (b) an external log file outside the event log forfeits the free cross-clone replication + compaction reuse the chosen approach inherits. The chosen reducer-ignored-sidecar is at-least-as-good on codebase-alignment, blast-radius, testability, and simplicity.

Positive rationale is stated ("Offline FP/remediation analysis without taxing rebar's hot paths"; "reuse rebar's existing event compaction"; the one-owner id rationale). G6 checklist all satisfied.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 review — rubric T8 (LLM/prompt structural-completeness probe, overlay)

The overlay applies: the plan defines part of an LLM/agent review system — a REVIEW_RESULT output schema, a per-criterion verdict vocabulary, finding fingerprints, and references to two sibling agents (orchestrator `ca03` and Pass-4 coach `75a9`). Tool-grounded the referenced agents/enums/schema/payload. ONE proven structural gap; the rest of the T8 surface is clean.

FINDING

- [ ] Define the per-pass LATENCY + COST metadata shape the added AC requires — schema location, value vocabulary, units, and per-pass keying.
  - criteria: [T8]
  - location: Plan §"Acceptance Criteria (added — close G3 coverage)", AC "Capture per-pass LATENCY + COST … in the sidecar metadata"
  - evidence: The AC names a metadata field ("per-pass LATENCY + COST", distinguishing "the fast claim-path check" vs "the out-of-band review") but never defines its shape. The implemented sidecar payload (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py:68-82`) carries `schema/verdict/ticket_id/ticket_type/material_fingerprint/model/runner/coverage/findings/coaching` — no latency or cost field. `coverage` in the verdict schema is a free-form object (`plan_review_verdict.schema.json:46-50`, `additionalProperties: true`) describing only "what ran" (DET coverage, routing, chunk count, llm_ran, verdict counts). An executor cannot tell WHERE these metrics live (a new top-level `metrics` field? a `coverage.latency`/`coverage.cost` sub-key?), the UNITS (wall-clock ms? input/output tokens? USD?), or how the two passes are KEYED so the offline join can separate the fast claim-path check from the out-of-band review. This is the T8 pattern — a metadata vocabulary referenced by an AC but never specified — so the "refined PASSIVELY from dogfood data" promise has no defined substrate.
  - suggested_fix: Add a `metrics` (or `coverage.metrics`) object to the sidecar payload schema with explicit per-pass keys and units, e.g. `{"pass": "det-floor"|"pass1-find"|"pass2-verify"|"pass3-decide"|"pass4-coach"|"claim-path-check", "wall_ms": int, "tokens": {"in": int, "out": int}, "usd": float|null}`, and state that the fast claim-path check and the out-of-band review are recorded as distinct keyed entries so offline analysis can separate them.

AFFIRMATIONS (T8 criteria this chunk passes)

- No undefined-but-referenced enum: the per-criterion VERDICT vocabulary the sidecar records is fully defined — `verdict` is PASS|BLOCK|INDETERMINATE (`plan_review_verdict.schema.json:10-13`), and the per-finding fields the sidecar slims (`id`, `criteria`, `tier`, `decision`, `severity`, `validity`, `impact`, `priority`, `reason`, `verification`) are enumerated in `sidecar.py:47-59`. "finding fingerprints" resolves to the content-fingerprint `id` minted by `orchestrator.mint_finding_id` (`ca03`).

- Referenced agents exist and are fully specified (tool-verified): orchestrator `ca03` (dry-frost-zenith, CLOSED) is the SOLE owner that MINTS the finding `id`; Pass-4 coach `75a9` (crusty-herb-stand) CONSUMES it via `finding_refs`; the sidecar RECORDS it. The id-ownership protocol is unambiguous and non-circular (mint-once/consume) — matching `ca03`'s What and `75a9`'s "Caller-visible finding id contract". No pink-elephant / instruction-locality antipattern: the sidecar records the id and does not re-mint it.

- No counter/state increment with ambiguous placement: idempotency placement is explicitly disambiguated — the sidecar is APPEND-ONLY per run (NOT idempotent, by design), while idempotency lives on the ATTESTATION head-state (one current valid signature). Exactly the kind of state-placement spec T8 looks for, and it is present.

- A fallback for a failed sub-step is specified: the sidecar emit is best-effort and "a failed emit must NEVER fail the review itself" (`sidecar.py:24-37` returns False on any exception) — the observability substrate degrades without taxing the hot path.

- The rollout/coexistence protocol referenced by the added AC is real and co-located with the schema: `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES` includes `REVIEW_RESULT` and `is_unknown_newer_type` distinguishes truly-newer types (`reducer/_version.py`); fsck WARNs on newer types; preserve-and-ignore + readers-before-writers documented at `sidecar.py:7-14`. No structural gap.

Net: one MAJOR-candidate structural gap (undefined latency/cost metadata vocabulary on a referenced schema field); the rest of the T8 surface is clean.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS 1 review (G6 — approach soundness). One mechanism-correctness finding on the retention path; the rest of the approach is sound and codebase-aligned (affirmed below).

- [ ] (G6) Reconcile the retention/cleanup AC's mechanism: it says "reuse rebar's existing event compaction" to bound REVIEW_RESULT growth, but existing compaction (`src/rebar/_commands/compact.py:74-85`) explicitly SKIPS any event whose type is not in `KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES`, and `REVIEW_RESULT` is deliberately excluded from that set (`src/rebar/reducer/_version.py:44,65-94`; `sidecar.py:7-14` states "compaction PRESERVES it … never absorbed into a SNAPSHOT"). The two requirements are in direct tension: the forward-compat preserve-and-ignore guarantee means existing compaction will never fold or delete a REVIEW_RESULT file, so it cannot bound sidecar growth as written. Specify the ACTUAL bounding mechanism (e.g. a dedicated age/size-based archival/prune pass for reducer-ignored sidecars, distinct from KNOWN-type SNAPSHOT compaction) so the "bounds growth while preserving recent history" AC has a real owner.

Affirmations (criteria G6 satisfies):
- Mechanism correctness: the reducer-ignored sidecar following the SYNC/PRECONDITIONS preserve-and-ignore precedent is correct and proven by tests (`test_event_schema_forward_compat.py`, `test_plan_review_gate.py:156-174`); fsck recognition via `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES` is wired (`_version.py:44`).
- Id-ownership data flow is sound and non-circular: orchestrator (`ca03`) is SOLE minter (`orchestrator.py:153-158`), sidecar RECORDS, coach (`75a9`) REFERENCES — one owner, no circular alignment, exactly as the plan's "What" states.
- Append-only-per-run vs. idempotency-on-attestation is a deliberate, correct separation (no TOCTOU: idempotency lives on head-state signature, not the append-only sidecar).
- Fitness-for-purpose: capture-only + offline join solves the named problem (per-criterion FP/FN analysis without taxing hot paths) directly, not via a proxy.
- Approach selection: a reviewer-generated alternative (fold verdicts into a KNOWN reduced field) would tax compiled state/hot paths and lose forward-compat preservation — the chosen reducer-ignored-sidecar approach is defensibly superior on blast-radius and codebase-alignment.
- Positive rationale present ("Why": offline analysis without taxing hot paths; "What": one-owner id rationale).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
## Completion Verification Result: FAIL

Two of the six acceptance criteria are not met in the implementation.

---

### AC1 — REVIEW_RESULT sidecar emitted with finding fingerprints; the reducer skips the type. ✅ MET

- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py` emits `REVIEW_RESULT` events with per-finding fingerprints (`id` via `mint_finding_id`, plus `criteria`, `tier`, `decision`, etc.).
- `REVIEW_RESULT` is absent from `KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES` (`src/rebar/reducer/_version.py:65-94`) so the reducer skips it, while it IS in `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES` (line 44) and the write allow-list (`src/rebar/_store/event_append.py:63`).

### AC2 — head / .cache / deps / validate are unaffected by sidecar size/count. ✅ MET

- Because `REVIEW_RESULT` is not in `KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES`, it never enters compiled state or hot paths; `compact.py:74-85` explicitly skips/preserves it.

### AC3 — Offline reconstruction of per-criterion FP/remediation is possible. ✅ MET

- `sidecar.py:40-82` builds a payload with all findings across blocking/advisory/overflow/indeterminate/dropped sets, each slimmed to `id`, `criteria`, `tier`, `decision`, `severity`, `validity`, `impact`, `priority`, `reason`, `verification`.
- The offline join pattern (ticket_id + finding id) is documented at `docs/plan-review-gate.md:183-190`.

### AC4 — A retention/cleanup path bounds sidecar growth while preserving recent history. ❌ NOT MET

- The ticket specifies "reuse rebar's existing event compaction" but `compact.py:74-85` does the **opposite**: it explicitly preserves (never deletes/snapshots) any event not in `KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES`. `REVIEW_RESULT` is intentionally excluded from `KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES`, so compaction will never bound its growth — by design it preserves these events indefinitely.
- No separate archival, prune, or age/size-based cleanup command for `REVIEW_RESULT` events exists anywhere in the codebase (`src/rebar/_commands/` has no such mechanism; a full search for `prune`, `archiv`, `retention`, `cleanup`, `bound.*sidecar` returned no matches).
- This gap was surfaced repeatedly in the ticket's own review comments but was never resolved in the implementation.

### AC5 (added) — Capture per-pass LATENCY + COST in the sidecar metadata. ❌ NOT MET

- `sidecar.py:68-82` defines the payload as: `schema`, `verdict`, `ticket_id`, `ticket_type`, `material_fingerprint`, `model`, `runner`, `coverage`, `findings`, `coaching`. No latency, wall_ms, cost, tokens, or metrics fields exist.
- `orchestrator.py:416-550` (`run_review`) captures no timing/cost data before calling sub-passes, and passes nothing to `sidecar.emit()`.
- `review_plan()` in `__init__.py:36-92` records no timing around `orchestrator.run_review(ctx, cfg, ...)`.
- The documentation acknowledges the intent ("Per-run latency/cost is captured on the sidecar for passive refinement", `docs/plan-review-gate.md:208`) but this is aspirational — the code does not implement it.

### AC6 — ROLLOUT / cross-clone coexistence. ✅ MET

- `REVIEW_RESULT` is in `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES` so `is_unknown_newer_type()` returns `False` for it (`_version.py:47-54`).
- `fsck.py:152-178` WARNs only on truly-unknown-newer types; REVIEW_RESULT is recognized and does not trigger the warning.
- `sidecar.py:7-14` documents the preserved-and-ignored-by-older-clones rollout pattern.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
## Completion Verification Result: PASS

All six acceptance criteria are met by the implementation. Evidence grounded in actual file reads below.

---

### AC1 — REVIEW_RESULT sidecar emitted with finding fingerprints; reducer skips the type. ✅ MET

- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py:22` — `EVENT_TYPE = "REVIEW_RESULT"`; `emit()` (lines 33–47) appends the event.
- `sidecar.py:92–104` — `_slim()` captures per-finding fingerprints: `id`, `criteria`, `tier`, `decision`, `severity`, `validity`, `impact`, `priority`, `reason`, `verification`.
- `src/rebar/reducer/_version.py:44` — `REVIEW_RESULT` is in `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES` but **not** in `KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES` (lines 65–94), so the reducer skips it.
- `src/rebar/_store/event_append.py:60–63` — `REVIEW_RESULT` is in the write-path allow-list so it can be emitted.

### AC2 — head / .cache / deps / validate unaffected by sidecar size/count. ✅ MET

- Because `REVIEW_RESULT` is absent from `KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES`, it is never replayed into compiled state; compaction preserves it untouched (`_version.py:9–17`). The hot paths are structurally isolated.

### AC3 — Offline reconstruction of per-criterion FP/remediation is possible. ✅ MET

- `sidecar.py:85–130` — `build_payload()` captures all finding sets (blocking, advisory, overflow, indeterminate, dropped), each slimmed to the fields needed for per-criterion offline join on `ticket_id + finding id`.

### AC4 — A retention/cleanup path bounds sidecar growth (dedicated `sidecar.prune`, NOT compaction). ✅ MET

- The Scope section (corrected) specifies a dedicated `rebar.llm.plan_review.sidecar.prune` — NOT compaction (which preserves reducer-ignored events by design).
- `sidecar.py:50–82` — `prune()` is implemented exactly as specified: lists `REVIEW_RESULT` files for the ticket sorted by filename-timestamp, keeps the most-recent `RETAIN_PER_TICKET = 10` (line 30), git-rm's older ones, and commits with a descriptive message.
- `sidecar.py:46` — `prune()` is called best-effort (exception-swallowing) after each successful `emit()`, off the reducer/compaction hot paths.

### AC5 — Capture per-pass LATENCY + COST proxy in sidecar metadata. ✅ MET

- `orchestrator.py:437` — `_t_total = time.monotonic()` starts total timing.
- `orchestrator.py:440–442` — `_t_det` / `det_ms` captures DET-floor latency.
- `orchestrator.py:463–465` — `_t_llm` / `llm_ms` captures LLM-pass latency.
- `orchestrator.py:551–559` — `coverage["metrics"]` is populated with `det_ms`, `llm_ms`, `total_ms`, `llm_calls`, and `claim_path` (a constant marker for the LLM/network-free fast claim path).
- `sidecar.py:121–123` — the sidecar payload lifts `coverage.metrics` into a top-level `"metrics"` field for easy offline join.

### AC6 — ROLLOUT / cross-clone coexistence. ✅ MET

- `src/rebar/reducer/_version.py:44` — `REVIEW_RESULT` ∈ `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES`; `is_unknown_newer_type("REVIEW_RESULT")` returns `False` (lines 47–54), so `fsck` does not false-warn on it.
- `sidecar.py:7–14` — documents the preserved-and-ignored-by-older-clones rollout pattern and readers-before-writers upgrade order.

---

All acceptance criteria are satisfied. Verdict: **PASS**.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
Offline FP/remediation analysis without taxing rebar's hot paths.

## What
- A reducer-IGNORED REVIEW_RESULT event capturing per-criterion verdicts + finding fingerprints + metadata +
  override justifications; the reducer skips the type; offline replay+join yields per-criterion fire/FP/FN rates.

- CALLER-VISIBLE FINDING ID: the stable per-finding `id` is MINTED by the orchestrator (`ca03`) at result assembly (the content fingerprint, sole owner); the sidecar RECORDS that same id/fingerprint and the Pass-4 coach (`75a9` crusty-herb-stand) references it (reference-not-restate) — one owner, no circular alignment, so the caller-facing reference and the offline-analysis record line up.
## Scope
- Capture only; analysis is offline; no human-feedback requirement.
- RETENTION/CLEANUP (T9/T5b): bound sidecar growth WITHOUT interfering with passive data-gathering. NOTE
  (corrected): because REVIEW_RESULT is reducer-IGNORED, rebar's event COMPACTION intentionally PRESERVES
  it (it never snapshots/absorbs a non-KNOWN type) — so compaction CANNOT bound it. Retention is instead a
  dedicated PRUNE (`rebar.llm.plan_review.sidecar.prune`) that keeps the most-recent RETAIN_PER_TICKET
  sidecars per ticket (recent history for offline FP/FN analysis) and removes older ones, run best-effort
  after each emit — off the reducer/compaction hot paths. The sidecar is intentionally APPEND-ONLY per
  review run (every run recorded for analysis — NOT idempotent, by design); idempotency lives on the
  ATTESTATION (head-state): re-running a review yields ONE current valid signature, not duplicates.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] REVIEW_RESULT sidecar emitted with finding fingerprints; the reducer skips the type.
- [ ] head / .cache / deps / validate are unaffected by sidecar size/count.
- [ ] Offline reconstruction of per-criterion FP/remediation is possible.
- [ ] A retention/cleanup path bounds sidecar growth while preserving recent history for offline analysis: a dedicated `sidecar.prune` (keep most-recent RETAIN_PER_TICKET per ticket, remove older), run best-effort after emit — NOT compaction (which preserves reducer-ignored events by design). Passive gathering uninterrupted.

## Acceptance Criteria (added — close G3 coverage)
- [ ] Capture per-pass LATENCY + a COST proxy in the sidecar metadata (`coverage.metrics` / payload `metrics`: det_ms, llm_ms, total_ms, llm_calls; the fast claim-path check is structurally LLM/network-free, recorded as a constant marker), so latency/cost targets set from UX/timeout projections are refined PASSIVELY from dogfood data (no upfront benchmark).
- [ ] ROLLOUT / cross-clone coexistence: the new `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar + the attestation head-state event follow rebar's preserved-and-ignored-by-older-clones pattern — an older clone predating the feature PRESERVES-and-IGNORES the event (never mis-reduces it), `fsck` WARNs on event types newer than the running binary, and reconcile hosts upgrade first (readers-before-writers).
compiled_state.title: REVIEW_RESULT reducer-ignored sidecar (observability substrate)
